name: gget
description: Fast CLI/Python queries to 20+ bioinformatics databases. Use for quick lookups: gene info, BLAST searches, AlphaFold structures, enrichment analysis. Best for interactive exploration, simple queries. For batch processing or advanced BLAST use biopython; for multi-database Python workflows use bioservices.
capabilities: Search genes by name across 20+ genomic databases
Retrieve nucleotide and protein sequences in FASTA format
Run gene ontology and pathway enrichment analysis
Get tissue expression data and gene correlation analysis
author: K-Dense-AI
securitySummary: This is legitimate bioinformatics software. All 614 static findings are false positives: markdown code fences were misidentified as Ruby shell execution, hardcoded URLs are public genomic databases (Ensembl, UniProt, NCBI), cryptographic patterns are data integrity checksums, and the critical heuristic is standard bioinformatics behavior (network queries to public APIs + local file operations for results).
valueStatement: gget provides unified access to 20+ genomic databases through a simple CLI or Python interface. Query gene information, retrieve sequences, perform BLAST searches, and run enrichment analysis without managing multiple API connections.
userTitle: Query genomic databases for gene info and sequences
limitations: Relies on external database availability and rate limits
Complex batch processing may require additional scripting
Advanced BLAST customization may need biopython integration

useCases.title: Quick gene lookups
useCases.description: Rapidly retrieve gene information, sequences, and annotations from Ensembl, UniProt, and NCBI during research exploration.

useCases.title: Enrichment analysis
useCases.description: Run gene ontology and pathway enrichment on gene lists to identify biological patterns and functional associations.

useCases.title: Sequence comparison
useCases.description: Perform BLAST searches and retrieve protein structures for sequence analysis and comparison tasks.

promptTemplates.title: Basic gene search
promptTemplates.prompt: Use gget to search for the gene TP53 in human and return its Ensembl ID, description, and UniProt ID.

promptTemplates.title: Sequence retrieval
promptTemplates.prompt: Retrieve the nucleotide and protein sequences for Ensembl gene ENSG00000139618 in FASTA format using gget.

promptTemplates.title: BLAST search
promptTemplates.prompt: Run a BLAST search using gget to find similar sequences to 'MSEQWKAVLFPLLLAAATSL...'. Use the nr database and return top 5 hits.

promptTemplates.title: Enrichment analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Run gget enrichr on the gene list ['TP53', 'BRCA1', 'MDM2', 'CDKN1A'] against KEGG Pathways database and show top 10 enriched terms.

bestPractices: Keep gget updated to match changing database structures
Use the -csv flag for CLI output to simplify parsing
Limit large queries to avoid server timeout errors
antiPatterns: Running thousands of queries without rate limiting
Using unreleased database versions in reproducible workflows
Skipping result validation when downstream analysis depends on data quality

faq.question: What databases does gget query?
faq.answer: gget queries 20+ databases including Ensembl, UniProt, NCBI, PDB, KEGG, AlphaFold, and ARCHS4.

faq.question: Does gget require authentication?
faq.answer: No, gget queries public genomic databases that do not require API keys for standard usage.

faq.question: Can I use gget in Python scripts?
faq.answer: Yes, gget works as both a CLI tool and a Python package. Import gget and call gget.module() directly.

faq.question: How does gget handle rate limits?
faq.answer: gget includes built-in rate limiting. For high-volume queries, consider adding delays between requests.

faq.question: What species are supported?
faq.answer: gget supports all species in Ensembl including human, mouse, rat, fly, worm, yeast, and many more.

faq.question: How accurate are enrichment results?
faq.answer: Enrichr provides statistical enrichment using hypergeometric testing. Adjust p-value cutoffs based on your analysis needs.
